name: Eisa Festival
traditions: Dynamic group dancing with taiko drums (paranku and odaiko); traditional Ryukyuan music; kachashi dance; community performances.
historicalBackground: Eisa dance originated in the 17th century as a Buddhist Obon observance unique to Okinawa. It blends Ryukyuan culture with mainland Japanese Bon Odori traditions.
description: Okinawa's electrifying drum dance festival, where troupes of dancers perform dynamic Eisa dances to honor ancestors during the Obon season.
